summary: Matched the common fighter throw unit ftCo_Throw by replacing many placeholder stubs with decompiled throw selection, throw-state setup, animation/physics/collision callbacks, throw damage/knockback application, and small helper wrappers. The PR marked ftCo_Throw.c as Matching in configure.py, adjusted GALE01 section splits for newly attributed .data/.sdata/.sdata2, and added the supporting type/prototype work needed by the match. The decomp.dev bot reported 26 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Throw, +4748 matched bytes and +4860 linked bytes.

key_files.why: Main matched unit; replaced ftCo_Throw placeholders with implemented throw helpers and callbacks.
Updated public prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures for matched throw functions.
Added ftCommon_MotionVars.throw fields used by throw code, including Vec xC.
Typed common data and fighter data fields needed for throw matching, including weight, weight_independent_throws_mask, x37C, x380, and Fighter.x2174.
Changed ftColl_80079AB0 from void/unknown to a typed float-returning knockback helper signature.
Declared typed ftColl_80079AB0 signature used by throw damage code.
Moved/introduced lbColl_80008D30_arg1 forward type so ft/types.h can reference it.
Removed the lbColl_80008D30_arg1 definition after moving it to lb/forward.h.
Adjusted data/sdata/sdata2 ownership around ftCo_Attack100 and ftCo_Throw.
Switched ftCo_Throw.c from NonMatching to Matching.

what_changed: Adjusted GALE01 splits so ftCo_Throw owns .data 0x803C70E0-0x803C70EA, .sdata 0x804D3D68-0x804D3D75, and .sdata2 0x804D9100-0x804D910C; ftCo_Attack100 data/sdata end addresses moved earlier.
Implemented ftCo_800DD100 and ftCo_800DD168 victim cleanup/ordering helpers around ftCo_800DC920, ftCo_800DCFD4, and ftCommon_8007D92C.
Implemented ftCo_800DD1E4 throw-direction selection using left stick/c-stick thresholds and motion IDs 219-222, then dispatching to ftCo_800DD4B0.

Implemented ftCo_800DD398 and ftCo_800DD4B0 to set throw motion state, reset throw flags/motion variables, compute victim motion ID and animation speed, handle Kirby/Samus special cases, and update collision state.
Added fn_800DD568, fn_800DD5EC, fn_800DD684, and fn_800DD6E4 helper callbacks for throw collision/landing/animation-rate paths.

Implemented shared throw update helper ftCo_800DD724 that consumes throw flag bits, flips facing when requested, applies release logic, freezes animation at command-variable timing, stores TransN offset, and syncs victim thrown state.
Implemented forward/back/high/low throw animation, IASA, physics, collision, and high-throw camera callbacks, with repeated collision logic across throw directions.
Implemented ftCo_800DDDE4 throw damage/knockback application and its small wrapper functions ftCo_800DE2A8 and ftCo_800DE2CC, plus ftCo_800DE2F0 self-damage/knockback logic.
Added typed throw motion variables to ftCommon_MotionVars and refined several fighter/common-data fields from UNK_T or filler to concrete float/int/Vec/struct types.
Typed ftColl_80079AB0 as returning float with Fighter, HitCapsule, count, attack/defense/weight parameters.
Moved lbColl_80008D30_arg1 typedef from lbcollision.h to lb/forward.h.
smart_moves: Factored direction-neutral throw behavior into ftCo_800DD724 and reused it from ThrowF/ThrowB/ThrowHi/ThrowLw animation callbacks instead of duplicating the command-variable and flag handling.
Used small inline helpers for threshold-edge checks in ftCo_800DD1E4, matching the compiler pattern while keeping the throw selection logic readable.

Kept special-case behavior localized: Kirby up-throw flag/camera handling, Samus grapple-beam creation, Bowser/Giga Bowser victim motion override for Peach/Zelda on motion 222, and Mewtwo/Fox-related throw animation hooks.
Introduced typed struct fields only where the matched code proved their layout and usage, such as ftCommon_MotionVars.throw and ftData weight/weight_independent_throws_mask.
Split ftCo_800DDDE4 into a fully parameterized core plus two bool wrapper entry points, preserving the address-level API while avoiding duplicated damage logic.
Moved lbColl_80008D30_arg1 to a forward header so shared ft types can refer to the collision parameter struct without keeping the definition in lbcollision.h.
decomp_lessons: When matching a fighter action unit, header/type cleanup is often part of the match: unknown prototypes, motion variables, common-data fields, and collision helper signatures may need to become typed together.
Repeated action-state callbacks can hide a common per-frame helper; identifying that helper makes multiple animation callbacks match with less duplicated code.
For throw code, watch for both actor and victim state: victim_gobj, x1A5C, x221B bitfields, victim motion IDs, and thrown-state sync functions all affect correctness and matching.
Use existing command variables and throw_flags bitfields carefully; this PR shows throw_flags_b3/b4 being consumed and reset at exact points.
Data/sdata split ownership can shift when a previously nonmatching unit becomes matching; update splits alongside configure.py rather than treating code matching as source-only.
Struct fields should be typed to the narrow evidence needed by the code: x10C and x37C as floats, x348 as int, x380 as lbColl_80008D30_arg1, Fighter.x2174 as Vec, and ftData.x0 weight fields.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as ftCo_800DD1E4, ftCo_800DDDE4, and fn_800DD568.
Public throw callbacks use action-direction names: ftCo_ThrowF_Anim, ftCo_ThrowF_IASA, ftCo_ThrowF_Phys, ftCo_ThrowF_Coll, and corresponding ThrowB/ThrowHi/ThrowLw variants.
Static compiler-shaping helpers were named with the parent function address and inline suffix, for example ftCo_800DD1E4_inline1 and ftCo_800DD724_inline2.
Newly identified motion-variable fields remain provisional x-prefixed names inside a semantic container: fp->mv.co.throw.x4, x8, xC.
Typed but still semantically unknown data fields keep x-offset names, e.g. p_ftCommonData->x37C and ftData.x0->weight_independent_throws_mask.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x10), PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(0x14), and PAD_STACK(4) in matched functions where stack shape required padding.
Used static inline helpers to reproduce branch/threshold patterns for stick-direction edge detection and throw flag consumption.
Preserved numeric motion IDs 219-222 for thrower motions and computed victim motion IDs by offsetting from 239, indicating the enum names were not yet introduced or not needed for the match.
Kept empty IASA callbacks as explicit return bodies rather than one-line empty functions, likely to match expected codegen/style for void no-op callbacks.
Matched wrapper functions ftCo_800DE2A8 and ftCo_800DE2CC by forwarding to ftCo_800DDDE4 with true/false rather than duplicating the core routine.
Changed include style in ftCo_Throw.c to explicit <melee/...> headers and added needed subsystem includes for ftanim, ftcoll, ftparts, mp collision, lb collision, and character-specific helpers.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 43.22% (+0.12%, +4748 bytes) and linked code 27.56% (+0.13%, +4860 bytes).
The bot listed 26 new 100% matches, all in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Throw, with the largest being ftCo_800DDDE4 at +1220 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_800DD1E4, ftCo_800DD398, ftCo_800DD4B0, or ftCo_800DDDE4 to semantic throw names.
Search for follow-up struct refinement of ftCommon_MotionVars.throw fields x0/x4/x8/xC and Fighter bitfields throw_flags_b3/throw_flags_b4.
Check whether motion IDs 219-222 and victim IDs 239-243 were later enumerated for common throws.
Look for PRs that complete or type ftColl_80079AB0, since this PR only changed its signature while leaving the implementation NOT_IMPLEMENTED.
Search for any cleanup moving lbColl_80008D30_arg1 out of forward.h or giving it a semantic name.
